text: Between 1951 and 1991 seventeen processes were developed for extraction of uranium from wet phosphoric acid (WPA). This development work was based on uranium market prices which varied over time and were therefore not always sufficient to obtain an economic process. Today, extraction of uranium from WPA appears to be an economic option, with the global uranium demand increasing because of the growing number of developing countries and hence the need for a revision of the existing technology with a view to possible improvements and even innovations. The extraction processes developed in the past for the recovery of uranium from wet phosphoric acid (WPA) and the physicochemical principles which they involve are summarised. Recent developments concerning new extraction systems are also presented and discussed.
